text:
BREAST PATHOLOGY REPORT
Patient ID: BP-2024-001
Date: 2024-03-15
Laterality: Left

CLINICAL HISTORY:
58-year-old female presenting with a palpable mass in the left upper outer quadrant detected on routine screening mammogram. Mass measures 2.1 cm on ultrasound. No axillary lymphadenopathy on imaging. Core needle biopsy performed.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Core needle biopsy material: Multiple grey-white cores measuring 1.2-1.5 cm in length.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show infiltrating carcinoma composed of cords and clusters of epithelial cells with moderate nuclear pleomorphism (grade 2). Nuclei are enlarged with prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are present (4/10 HPF). Tumour cells are set in a desmoplastic stroma. No lymphovascular invasion identified in the submitted material. No lobular architecture. No associated in situ component identified in the submitted material.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
ER: Strongly positive (>90% nuclei, H-score 280)
PR: Positive (60% nuclei, H-score 140)
HER2: 1+ (negative)
Ki-67: 15%

DIAGNOSIS:
Left breast, core needle biopsy:
- Invasive breast carcinoma of no special type, histological grade 2 (Nottingham score 6/9)
- ER positive, PR positive, HER2 negative (Luminal A-like)

COMMENT:
The morphological and immunohistochemical features are those of a hormone receptor-positive, HER2-negative invasive breast carcinoma of no special type. Correlation with clinical and radiological findings is recommended.

BREAST PATHOLOGY REPORT
Patient ID: BP-2024-002
Date: 2024-03-22
Laterality: Right

CLINICAL HISTORY:
26-year-old female with a 6-month history of a slowly growing, well-defined right breast mass. Ultrasound demonstrates a 3.2 cm oval, homogeneous, hypoechoic lesion with circumscribed margins, BI-RADS 3. Core needle biopsy performed for reassurance.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Core needle biopsy material: Four cream-white cores measuring 1.3-1.6 cm in length. Tissue is firm with a whorled appearance on cut surface.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a biphasic lesion composed of a proliferation of intralobular stroma surrounding and compressing benign epithelial elements. The stromal cells are bland, spindled, and arranged in a pericanalicular and intracanalicular pattern. Epithelial elements are represented by compressed glandular structures lined by a double cell layer. No cytological atypia is identified. Mitoses are absent in both the stroma and epithelium. No necrosis. No malignant elements identified.

DIAGNOSIS:
Right breast, core needle biopsy:
- Fibroadenoma

COMMENT:
The morphological appearances are entirely consistent with a fibroadenoma. The lesion is well-circumscribed and the stromal and epithelial components are both benign. No atypical or malignant features are identified. Clinical and radiological follow-up as per local protocol. Excision not required on pathological grounds.

BREAST PATHOLOGY REPORT
Patient ID: BP-2024-003
Date: 2024-04-01
Laterality: Left

CLINICAL HISTORY:
47-year-old female with screen-detected microcalcifications in the left breast, upper inner quadrant. Stereotactic vacuum-assisted biopsy performed. No palpable abnormality. Family history: mother with breast cancer at age 62.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Vacuum-assisted biopsy cores: Multiple cream-white cores measuring 0.8-1.4 cm in length. Some cores contain gritty foci on cut surface consistent with calcification.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show ducts distended by a proliferation of atypical epithelial cells arranged in a solid and cribriform pattern with central necrosis and associated dystrophic calcification (comedo-type necrosis). The neoplastic cells exhibit high nuclear grade with pleomorphic, hyperchromatic nuclei and prominent nucleoli. Mitoses are easily identified. A continuous myoepithelial cell layer is present at the periphery of involved ducts, confirmed on p63 immunostaining. No evidence of invasive carcinoma in the submitted material.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
ER: Strongly positive (95% nuclei)
PR: Focally positive (15% nuclei)
HER2: 3+ (positive, score confirmed by reflex FISH)
p63: Highlights intact myoepithelial layer
Ki-67: 45%

DIAGNOSIS:
Left breast, vacuum-assisted biopsy:
- Ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS), high nuclear grade, solid and cribriform pattern with comedo-type necrosis
- Associated microcalcification present
- No invasive carcinoma identified

COMMENT:
High-grade DCIS with HER2 amplification. The absence of invasion in the biopsy material does not exclude invasion elsewhere. Surgical excision with sentinel lymph node biopsy is recommended.
